passedChecks.description: Two-column layout with tabs on left, content panel on right matches Figma structure exactly. Tablist column and content panel are flex-1 split (50/50).
Accordion layout with details/summary elements. Each tab item renders as a collapsible accordion with plus/minus icon. Layout correctly stacks vertically.
Tab buttons have correct min-h-[140px] matching Figma h-[140px]. Gap between buttons is gap-4 (16px) matching Figma gap-[16px]. Tab column has py-16 (64px) matching Figma py-[64px].
Tab buttons have correct border-l border-t border-b (left, top, bottom only - no right border) matching Figma. Border radius is rounded-l-3xl (24px) which is within 1px tolerance of Figma's 25px.

Active tab background uses bg-accent which maps to the dynamic --accent-color CSS variable. Content panel uses bg-accent-lighter which correctly maps to the lighter shade. Colors match Figma: yellow = #ffd24c for active, #ffedb7 for panel; blue = #47cbf2 for active, #b5eafa for panel.
Tab button title uses font-heading (Anton) with text-header-3 md:text-[2.5rem] (40px at desktop). Figma specifies font-size 40px (font/size/3xl). Match confirmed.

Tab button title has leading-none which produces line-height: 1 on the title text. Figma shows leading-[1.1]. The difference is minimal (within tolerance for heading text that is typically single-line at desktop).
Content panel has rounded-3xl (24px) matching Figma rounded-[25px] within 1px tolerance. Panel padding is p-6 (24px) matching Figma p-[24px].
Description on active tab correctly shows with mt-6 (24px margin-top) creating visual separation from the title. Animation uses max-h transition with opacity for smooth reveal/hide.
Sticky positioning on tab column: md:sticky md:top-[calc(var(--fixed-elements-height)+32px)] md:self-start. This ensures the tablist stays visible while scrolling content panel.
Tab items (details elements) also have sticky positioning: md:sticky md:top-[calc(var(--fixed-elements-height)+32px)] md:self-start. This keeps the active content panel visible.
Hover states implemented: hover:bg-accent-light on inactive tab buttons, hover:!bg-accent on selected tab button. This matches the expected behavior from the design.
Mobile accordion items have border border-charcoal rounded-3xl (24px) matching Figma rounded-[25px] within 1px. Full border on all sides, correct for mobile.

Mobile accordion active state uses open:bg-accent open:border-accent, correctly coloring the open accordion item with the accent color. The desktop version correctly removes this with md:open:bg-transparent.
Summary element has hover:bg-accent-light for closed items and group-open:hover:bg-accent for open items, providing appropriate hover feedback.
Mobile accordion title uses font-heading text-header-3 which at mobile resolves to 32px (2rem). Figma mobile shows text-[32px] (font/size/2xl). Match confirmed.
Mobile accordion items have gap-4 (16px) between them matching Figma gap-[16px]. Summary padding is p-6 (24px) matching Figma p-[24px].

ARIA attributes are correctly applied: tablist has role='tablist' with aria-orientation='vertical', tab buttons have role='tab' with aria-selected and aria-controls, content panels have role='tabpanel' with aria-labelledby.

issues.observation: The tab button title uses leading-none which produces line-height:1. Figma specifies leading-[1.1]. For single-line tab titles this difference is invisible. For multi-line titles (e.g., 'Financial Wellbeing + Support'), it produces slightly tighter line spacing. Visually in the screenshots the text appears nearly identical to the Figma reference.
issues.recommendation: Change leading-none to leading-[1.1] on the tabs-button-title span in both Tabs.php (line 50) and Tabs.tsx (lines 152, 175) for exact match. Low priority as visual impact is minimal.

change.from: tabs-button-title font-heading text-header-3 md:text-[2.5rem] leading-none w-full
change.to: tabs-button-title font-heading text-header-3 md:text-[2.5rem] leading-[1.1] w-full

change.from: tabs-button-title font-heading text-header-3 md:text-[2.5rem] leading-none w-full
change.to: tabs-button-title font-heading text-header-3 md:text-[2.5rem] leading-[1.1] w-full

issues.figmaValue: gap-[24px] between title and body text in content panel
issues.observation: The desktop title inside the content panel (tabs-item-desktop-title) has mb-8 (32px margin-bottom) but the Figma design context shows the gap between the title and body content is 24px (gap-[24px]). The 8px difference is visually noticeable as extra space below the content panel heading.
issues.recommendation: Change mb-8 to mb-6 (24px) on the desktop title in both TabsItem.php (line 30) and TabsItem.tsx (line 108).

change.from: tabs-item-desktop-title hidden md:block font-heading text-header-3 md:text-[2.5rem] mb-8
change.to: tabs-item-desktop-title hidden md:block font-heading text-header-3 md:text-[2.5rem] mb-6

change.from: tabs-item-desktop-title hidden md:block font-heading text-header-3 md:text-[2.5rem] mb-8
change.to: tabs-item-desktop-title hidden md:block font-heading text-header-3 md:text-[2.5rem] mb-6

notes.description: Border radius: Implementation uses rounded-l-3xl (24px) vs Figma's 25px. The 1px difference is within tolerance and visually imperceptible.
Content panel border radius: Implementation uses rounded-3xl (24px) vs Figma's 25px. The 1px difference is within tolerance.

The second Figma desktop reference (807:58405, blue variant) shows items-center on the tab layout row, meaning the content panel vertically centers its content when shorter than the tab column. The implementation uses items-stretch which makes the panel fill the full height. The visual difference is only apparent with very short content. The items-stretch approach is more robust for content of varying lengths.

The text-header-3 utility provides font-family, font-size, line-height, and font-weight as a bundle. It is overridden at md with md:text-[2.5rem] (40px). The default text-header-3 line-height is 1.2, which gets overridden by the explicit leading-none. Figma specifies 1.1 line-height. The implementation should ideally use leading-[1.1] to match exactly.

observations: Two-column layout renders correctly with tablist on left, content panel on right
Active tab (first by default) shows accent background color with description revealed via mt-6 animation
Inactive tabs show border-only styling with min-h-[140px] constraint
Content panel has accent-lighter background with rounded corners and proper padding
Content panel shows tab title, rich text body, and sub-sections with dividers
Multiple block variations display correctly: yellow (4 tabs), blue (3 tabs), minimal (2 tabs), many tabs (8 tabs), long content, green accent
Sticky positioning works for both tablist and content panels
Same desktop layout applies at 1024px (above md breakpoint at 960px/60rem)
Columns are slightly narrower but proportions are maintained
Tab button titles wrap correctly with leading-none on multi-word titles
All interactive elements remain functional
Layout switches to mobile accordion mode (below md breakpoint)
Each tab renders as a details/summary accordion element
Accordion items have full border (border border-charcoal) and rounded-3xl corners
Plus/minus toggle icon renders correctly in top-right
Active/open accordion shows accent background color
Summary has correct hover states: hover:bg-accent-light for closed, group-open:hover:bg-accent for open
Content area shows with proper padding (px-6 pb-6 pt-2)
Accordion items stack vertically with gap-4 between them
Title font size is text-header-3 which resolves to 32px at mobile, matching Figma's text-[32px]
Rounded corners are fully visible on all accordion items
Overflow is correctly hidden (overflow-hidden on details element) preventing content from breaking border-radius
Open accordion shows content including description and discourse blocks
Section padding matches expected mobile values
